description: The National Testing Agency has cancelled the NEET-UG 2026 examination. This decision follows evidence of a significant overlap between circulated guess papers and the actual question paper. The Central Bureau of Investigation will now conduct a comprehensive probe into the alleged paper leak and malpractice network. Over 24 lakh aspirants are affected by this unprecedented move.

description: Another woman has died from post-cesarean section complications at Kota's New Medical College Hospital, marking the fourth fatality in a week. Pinki Mahawar, 30, succumbed after being transferred from JK Lon Hospital, with her husband alleging a delayed referral. The incident has led to protests and calls for accountability regarding safety protocols in government hospitals.

title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs and CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making priorities clear for all employees
description: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: A US family sold their company for $1.7 bn and gave 540 workers $240 mn of it
description: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.
